text:
The Olympic Games are now in full swing and Team GB have made a solid start.

Equestrians Laura Collett, Tom McEwen and Ros Canter secured Team GB's first gold medal of the Olympics on Monday by winning the eventing team event.

Hours later Tom Pidcock snatched a second gold with an outrageous late overtake in the men's mountain bike race - and he even suffered a puncture earlier in the battle.

It follows up success on the opening day, where Team GB won a bronze and silver - their first opening day medals since 2004.

Simone Biles is also beaten on the floor as she's forced to settle for silver.

She still ends the Paris Games with four medals (of which three are golds), taking her career total to 11 overall.

An epic comeback after her miserable Tokyo Olympics.

Ashock in the balance beam finalas Simone Biles falls and her performance sees her finish fifth.

It's been a slow day for Team GB so far - Molly Caudery and Holly Bradshaw failed to qualify for the pole vault.

Still just the bronze from the mixed relay triathlon.

A bronze for Team GB today in the mixed relay triathlon after a video review.

It was thought Beth Potter had pipped USA's Taylor Knibb at the line to clinch silver.

But the footage has been reviewed and the GB medal has been downgraded from silver to bronze.

Nonetheless still a cracking effort from Georgia Taylor-Brown, Alex Yee and Sam Dickinson.

Noah Lyles won gold for USA in a thrilling 100m final.

It proved to be a photo finish, with Lyles and his rivals learning their fate by watching the replays on the big screen.

Lyles pipped Jamaica's Kishane Thompson by five-thousandths of a second, recording a personal best of 9.79. seconds.

China have secured another gold with a great swim in the 4x400m medley relay final. Pan Zhanle produced an epic final leg for his country.

But for Team GB, they were forced to settle with fourth place.

Tommy Fleetwood produced a great perfomance at Le Golf National, but was forced to settle for silver as Scottie Scheffler put on a show to finish one shot ahead.

It's another medal however for Team GB, and he can be very proud of his performance.

Meanwhile, Hideki Matsuyama of Japan claimed bronze.

Novak Djokovichas claimed Olympic gold by beatingCarlos Alcarazin straight sets in the men's finall.

A great performance from the Serb, who was visibly emotional after sealing his first gold medal.

Heartbreak for Alcaraz.

The first medal of the day for Great Britain comes in equestrian.

Lottie Fry wins bronze aboard Glamourdale in the individual dressage.

A second dressage medal of these Games for Team GB.

USA have won gold and taken the 4x100m medley relay world record off Team GB.

Team GB finished seventh in the race this evening, a disappointing result.

Julien Alfred made history as she won the 100m women's final, becoming St Lucia's first-ever medaliist at the Olympics.

She finished ahead of US star Sha'Carri Richardson. Team GB's Daryll Neita was agonisingly close to the podium, finishing 0.04 behind Melissa Jefferson.

Team GB have claimed another bronze, this time in the 4x400m mixed relay.

A great effort from Samuel Reardon, Laviai Nielsen, Alex Haydock-Wilson and Amber Anning.

Simone Biles has won a seventh Olympic gold medal of her illustrious career, and her third at the Paris Games.

The US gymnastics icon produced a sensational performance in the women's vault final. She received a first score of 15.7 before a safer 14.9 combined for an overall total of 15.3.

Jake Jarman has secured his first Olympic medal - sealing bronze in the men's floor final.

A great performance from the Team GB star. Meanwhile, after a very strong performance, Luke Whitehouse came home in sixth.

Another bronze in dressage.

GB can't manage a equestrian clean sweep as Germany take gold.

But it's still a great effort from Becky Moody, Carl Hester and Lottie Fry considering the Charlotte Dujardin drama they had to deal ahead of these Games.

Denmark take silver.

The first medals of the day for Team GB come in the rowing!

The women's eights win bronze.

And the men's follow that up with gold, beating the Netherlands and the USA. They won by over a second.

That puts GB on 10 golds in Paris.

Ben Proud and Duncan Scott claimed silver medals tonight but the latter could not finish past the impressive Leon Marchand.

The Frenchman has won his fourth gold of the Paris Games tonight, on a strong day for the hosts who have moved to second in the medal table.

A third gold of the day for Team GB - this timeit's in the showjumping.

Ben Maher, Harry Charles and Scott Brash edged out USA which means GB join the Americans on nine golds at the Games overall.

Another gold for Team GB, the second of the day.

It comes from a tearful Bryony Page in the gymnastics.

The 33-year-old produced an incredible performance in the women's trampoline final.

She adds to her bronze from Tokyo 2021 and silver from Rio 2016.

Another gold for Team GB - the seventh of the Games!

Emily Craig and Imogen Grant win the last ever lightweight double sculls in Olympics history.

A bronze medal in the diving for Team GB!

China take gold in the 3m synchro diving with Mexico earning silver.

Very well earned from GB duo Jack Laugher and Anthony Harding as they earn the nation's fourth diving medal from four events in Paris.

That makes it the best Games EVER for Great Britain in terms of diving.

Heartbreak for Team GBin the men's pair.

Tom George and Ollie Wynne-Griffith are pipped to gold by Croatia in the final few seconds.

They still take home silver though.

Summer McIntosh has won her second gold medal in Paris, setting an Olympics record in the 200m butterfly with a time of 2:03.03.

What a performance from the 17-year-old Canadian. Her victory was met by some noise inside the arena.

Simone Biles produced the perfect performance to win gold in the all-around final in Paris.

She held her nerve at the end, seeing off both Brazil's Rebeca Andrade and USA's Sunisa Lee.

It's her sixth Olympic gold medal, and ninth overall.

Team GB star Joe Clarke has agonisingly missed out on an Olympics medal in the canoe slalom.

Clarke, who went last, finished fifth in the men's K1 final. Italy's Giovanni de Gennaro has claimed the gold medal.

The medals are pouring in now for Great Britain from the rowing lake.

The men's four take a convincing third place finish in the final to secure bronze, as the USA hold off New Zealand to win a first gold in this event for over 60 years.

An agonising near photo-finish sees the Team GB collect silver in the women's four.

Olympic legend Helen Glover narrowly misses out on sealing a record gold medal as the Netherlands win it at the last.

In third place, taking bronze, is the New Zealand boat.

A race for the ages!Silver for our women's four at the Vaires-sur-Marne \ud83e\udd48#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/ZAJ5tNFz1t

Another medal on the board for Great Britain, as Team GB continue their prowess in the rowing.

Mathilda Hodgkins Byrne and Rebecca Wilde claim bronze in the women's double sculls after a resilient effort.

New Zealand take gold, and defending champions Romania come in second for silver.

It's been a great day for Team GB with five medals including two golds.

It might've been an even better night for France.

Swimming superstar Leon Marchand has won two golds in one night to take his tally to three for these Games overall.

He wins the 200m breaststroke after winning the 200m butterfly just hours ago torecord an iconic doublethat Michael Phelps never even attempted previously.

Lauren Henry, Hannah Scott, Lola Anderson and Georgie Brayshaw win gold for Team GB in the women's quad sculls final.

It required a photo finish as the Dutch led for the majority of the race but Team GB fought back.

Read more here

Alex Yee has upgraded his silver medal from Tokyo to take gold in Paris.

The Team GB star was trailing leader Hayden Wilde by 15 seconds in the 10km race but fought back to win gold at the final stretch.

Read more here

It's a fourth gold medal for Team GB.

James Guy, Tom Dean, Matt Richards and Duncan Scott are triumphant in the 4x200m final.

Nathan Hales has won a third gold medal for Team GB.

It is Britain's first gold medal in the trap shooting since 1968.

The 28-year-old set a new Olympic record by hitting 40 of his 50 attempts.

The world record stands at 49 out of 50.

Serbia bag their first ever gold in shooting and it's the first gold medal given out today.

Zorana Arunovic and Damir Mikec hug each other and drop to the ground as they win the top gong in the 10m air pistol mixed team final.

Turkey take silver and India bronze.

The mixed team air pistol medal matches are currently underway and Turkey are in with a shot of a medal.
